name: Sheppard Bus
recent_news: Founder Kenneth Sheppard died July 18, 2025 at age 98; company remains family-owned/operated by son John and Kelly Sheppard (2nd generation, 4 children potentially 3rd gen).
fit_notes: Strong tuck-in candidate: South NJ family-owned operator with sizable ~500-vehicle fleet (school + charter + bus sales/leasing) operating in 5 NJ counties. Founder just passed (July 2025), with 2nd-gen John/Kelly Sheppard at helm — possible succession inflection point. NJ is a Summit core/expansion state. Note: '12 employees' in source data appears to refer to a small subsidiary entity; true headcount is significantly larger.

name: Middledorf Bus Co
districts_or_customers: Washington DC Metropolitan area schools (specific districts not disclosed publicly)
recent_news: No M&A news; FMCSA 'Conditional' safety rating from 2012 compliance review; 0 reported crashes in trailing 24 months. Stable small operator.
fit_notes: Small but durable family-run DC-metro school-bus contractor (12 buses, ~45 years operating since 1981). Owner Cris Middledorf and Diana Middledorf likely approaching retirement age (44+ yr tenure) — plausible succession-driven sale. MD is in-Summit-state. Size is below Summit's typical sweet spot — more of a bolt-on/tuck-in than a platform addition; legacy 'Conditional' safety rating is a diligence flag.

name: Secure Medical Transport
districts_or_customers: Kansas City metro healthcare and behavioral health facilities
recent_news: Active member of NEMTAC (Non-Emergency Medical Transportation Accreditation Commission) Advisory Board; profiled in 'Thinking Bigger' magazine; ZoomInfo notes coverage. No public M&A activity.
fit_notes: Mid-sized KC-metro NEMT operator (~$2.8M rev, 40 employees) founded 2009 by Kevin Teasdale (son of former MO Gov. Joseph Teasdale). Multi-segment platform (NEMT + behavioral health + school + government) is attractive but NEMT-centric — adjacent to Summit's core school-bus focus. Founder still actively engaged (NEMTAC Advisory Board) so succession signal is low. Better fit if Summit is building a special-needs / specialty-student-transport vertical in Missouri.

name: Georgia Medical Trnsprtn Inc
principals: Joe Hale (Owner per source data; 'J.H.' on LeadIQ)
districts_or_customers: Clarke, DeKalb, Fulton, Greene, Gwinnett, Hall, Habersham, Hart, Madison, and Oglethorpe counties (eastern metro Atlanta)
recent_news: No public M&A news. Note: dba/web presence is ambiguous — source lists website 'Guardianangeltransport.Com' but that domain appears to belong to a separate 'Guardian Angel Transport Service LLC' in NC/SC; LeadIQ lists Georgia Medical Transportation's website as gamedtransport.com (HQ also referenced as Bogart, GA, vs. Loganville address).
fit_notes: Pure-play NEMT operator (ambulatory + wheelchair) in eastern metro Atlanta; ~45 vehicles is meaningful for a private GA NEMT, but no school-bus contracts confirmed. Off-thesis for Summit's school-transportation platform unless Summit is intentionally adding NEMT/SPED-adjacent service line in GA. Website discrepancy and owner confirmation gap warrant a phone call before any approach.

name: Handi-Wheels Transportation
districts_or_customers: City of Fargo (FM RideSource paratransit partner)
recent_news: Long-running nonprofit paratransit agency; partners with City of Fargo FM RideSource. No M&A relevance.
fit_notes: NOT a fit — 501(c)(3) nonprofit paratransit agency (not a privately-owned operating contractor), tiny 4-vehicle fleet, no school-bus contracts, North Dakota out-of-footprint. Should be removed from Summit's target list.
